channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: 'Genuinely might be the worst trailer I've ever seen': new Netflix movie Ladies First slammed by outraged fans as woman dominated world becomes man's 'worst nightmare'
item.description: New Netflix movie Ladies First explores and man's worst nightmare in a woman dominated world — but its first trailer hasn't gone down well.

item.title: Samsung issues warning about fake SSDs in Europe — as the PC crisis sparks a massive slump in CPU sales
item.description: More fake SSDs and a CPU sales slump — the casualties of the RAM crisis are piling up, sadly.

item.title: The Best MagSafe Accessories (for Android, Too!): Chargers, Wallets, and More
item.description: MagSafe accessories make your phone feel uniquely yours. These are our favorites, including Android-friendly Qi2 picks.

item.title: What I learned by vibe-coding my own word processor
item.description: At first, I wasn’t positive I could make it work. Then I did. Now I can’t imagine life without it. Hello again, and welcome back to Fast Company’s Plugged In.

item.title: A giant cell tower is going to space this weekend
item.description: This weekend's scheduled Blue Origin rocket launch is rather momentous. Success would signal an end to SpaceX's monopoly on reusable orbital launch vehicles, and set up a three-way race to make that "No Service" indicator on your phone disappear forever. On Sunday morning, Jeff Bezos' massive New Glenn rocket is scheduled to launch with the […]

item.title: The Morning After: Our verdict on the DJI Osmo Pocket 4
item.description: You’ve probably seen DJI’s stick vlogging cameras everywhere. At trade shows and tech events I’ve attended, it’s often the de facto video camera used by reporters and influencers alike. The Osmo Pocket 3 was easy to use, had sharp focus, potent image stabilization and handled vertical and horizontal video recording with minimal compromises. That was two years ago, so naturally it’s time for an update. According to our review by James Trew, the Osmo Pocket 4 is the one to beat. It’s still incredibly easy to record with, whether you’re doing talk-to-camera vlogs or ambitious, more cinematic-qual...

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra : le meilleur smartphone de 2025 reste toujours aussi recommandable, surtout à moins de 1 000 €
item.description: Le Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra est l'ancien flagship du géant sud-coréen. Certes, le S26 Ultra vient d'être lancé, mais ce smartphone qu'on a adoré l'an dernier propose encore des performances de haut niveau. La bonne nouvelle, c'est qu'il est en promo à 999 euros au lieu de 1 469 euros.

item.title: n8n vs OpenClaw: What are the differences and where should you use either of them?
item.description: Where is operational tooling going?

item.title: ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’ Has the First Opening Credits for a ‘Star Wars’ Movie, and They May Hold a Major Clue
item.description: We thought 'The Bear' star Jeremy Allen White voicing Rotta the Hutt in 'Star Wars' was a small role, but, apparently, it's not.

item.title: How Can Astronauts Tell How Fast They’re Going?
item.description: Weirdly, spaceships have no direct way to gauge their own speed. Luckily, we can use some physics tricks to figure it out.

item.title: Canyon Spectral:ON CF 8 Electric Mountain Bike: Beginner-Friendly, Under $5K
item.description: The Spectral:ON CF 8 is a do-it-all, full-carbon electric mountain bike with an 800-Wh battery and under $4,500. Yes, please!

item.title: America’s gambling rehab crisis
item.description: There are more gambling apps than gambling rehab centers. Digital sportsbooks like DraftKings and FanDuel innovated us into an addiction epidemic. Can we muster the will—and the funding—to innovate ourselves out of this mess? It’s sometime after midnight on a Monday morning when Zach unlocks his phone and starts scrolling for something to bet on. He’s 26, tucked into his childhood bed at his parents’ house in Washington, D.C. He moved back in after a stint in Las Vegas that didn’t go as planned. The NFL is done for the night. The NBA’s late games have wrapped. Mainstream sports are fast asleep...

item.title: 007 First Light has its own Bond theme by award-winning singer Lana Del Rey
item.description: Lana Del Rey, the award-winning singer-songwriter, has been revealed as the artist behind 007 First Light's official theme song.

item.title: Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising Elite Trainer Box preorders are live — buy for under market price at Walmart
item.description: Preorder the Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising Elite Trainer Box for $114.99 at Walmart, beating TCGplayer’s market price.

item.title: Jusqu’à 36 euros de taxe copie privée sur votre prochain PC ou smartphone : ce qui vous attend
item.description: Les industries culturelles ont présenté leurs nouvelles exigences pour la redevance sur la copie privée lors d'une réunion de la commission copie privée au ministère de la Culture, le 15 avril. Les montants réclamés sur les téléphones et les tablettes s'envolent, et les ordinateurs pourraient être taxés pour la toute première fois. Cette offensive tarifaire promet de peser sur le portefeuille des consommateurs....

item.title: Solving the shadow IT crisis in travel
item.description: AI and shadow IT threaten travel security. Business leaders need to update policies and improve user-friendliness.

item.title: The Influencers Normalizing Not Having Sex
item.description: From a celibate porn star to an asexual ex-Mormon, the internet is full of people who are abstaining from sex—and it’s not just incels.

item.title: Agentic swarms will change how everyone uses AI – but how can organizations deploy them securely?
item.description: Autonomous agentic swarms promise massive productivity gains if organizations can maintain control and compliance.

item.title: Claude wants your passport as identity verification to access 'certain capabilities' — but promises it isn't using your face to train its models
item.description: Claude users will soon need to use government ID to prove who they are and use the platform.

item.title: Boulanger fait chuter de 150 € le TCL 65C79K, un TV 4K 144 Hz immersive de 65 pouces
item.description: Le TCL 65C79K s’impose comme un TV 4K particulièrement séduisant, pensé pour offrir une expérience visuelle à la fois fluide et lumineuse grâce à sa dalle QD-Mini LED et son taux de rafraîchissement de 144 Hz. Et la bonne nouvelle, c’est que ce modèle devient en tombant à 649 euros chez Boulanger au lieu de 799 euros.

item.title: The Roku Streaming Stick 4K Plus is back on sale at Amazon — save $10 right now
item.description: Find the best streaming stick deal. Save 25% on the Roku Streaming Stick 4K Plus at Amazon.

item.title: Lenovo ThinkStation P3 Ultra SFF Gen 2 review: A massive workstation smashed inside a mini PC
item.description: The Lenovo ThinkStation P3 Ultra SFF Gen 2 successfully crammed a full-size desktop into a compact chassis, but in doing so, the fan noise is quite impressive.

item.title: Stephen Colbert reacts to Pete Hegseth quoting the Bible. Sorry, quoting ‘Pulp Fiction’.
item.description: After defense secretary Pete Hegseth repeated an air rescue group's prayer that borrows from 'Pulp Fiction', Stephen Colbert had thoughts.

item.title: A new ‘Pixel Glow’ feature might be the Google Pixel 11’s secret weapon
item.description: The Google Pixel 11 series might feature RGB lights on the back to alert you of notifications.

item.title: Spotify se met à vendre de vrais livres, sauf en France
item.description: La plateforme de streaming musical prend un nouveau pari en s’ouvrant à l’achat de livres physiques depuis son application. Pour l’occasion, elle annonce un partenariat pour soutenir des librairies indépendantes.

item.title: 'Jesus!': 10,000 drones flock in Texas to tell the story of Jesus of Nazareth — and crush four world records
item.description: Record-breaking Texas drone show uses 10,000 drones to depict the life of Jesus and sets four Guinness World Records for massive aerial formations.

item.title: iPhone 17 Pro : en Chine, des réparateurs font des miracles sur son châssis en aluminium
item.description: L'iPhone 17 Pro Max n'est pas indestructible, surtout depuis qu'Apple a réadopté l'aluminium pour son cadre. Alors quand il tombe, c'est la panique... sauf si vous êtes en Chine.

item.title: Stellantis pourrait ouvrir ses usines européennes aux constructeurs chinois : le retour d’une alliance vieille de 30 ans
item.description: Stellantis pourrait ouvrir ses usines européennes au chinois Dongfeng, si on en croit plusieurs rumeurs, mais aussi profiter des usines chinoises de son partenaire pour vendre des voitures électriques badgées Stellantis dans plusieurs marchés.

item.title: The Pokémon TCG Perfect Order Booster Box is finally back in stock at Amazon — save $10
item.description: Amazon has the Pokémon TCG Perfect Order Elite Trainer Box back in stock for $83.90, with lower prices at Walmart and TCGplayer.

item.title: L’astuce anti-crise : comment cet adaptateur permet de réutiliser un SSD Xbox sur votre PC
item.description: À l'heure où accroître l'espace de stockage physique de votre PC peut vite coûter une petite fortune, toutes les combines sont bonnes à prendre. Celle dont nous allons vous parler est plutôt maline : elle permet de réutiliser la carte d'extension de stockage d'une Xbox Series directement sur un PC.

item.title: They bought property in the metaverse. Then it collapsed
item.description: A once-booming market for virtual real estate left early believers holding assets with little demand. Five years ago, tech angel investor Chris Adamo and a few friends jumped on a burgeoning trend in the digital asset world: they used a virtual real estate broker to buy 23 parcels of property in a metaverse called The Sandbox. He doesn’t remember exactly how much he spent, but it was around $200,000 for the whole group. The real estate, to be clear, consisted of pixelated parcels in “hip,” “trendy” virtual “neighborhoods,” an asset that crypto bros and Web3 enthusiasts like Adamo saw as the fu...

item.title: Speed isn’t strategy: Human judgement must be central to AI-led decisions
item.description: Organizations can get the best from AI by treating it less as a standalone decision-maker and more as a strategic partner: one that elevates insight and accelerates execution, while humans retain ownership of intent and outcome.

item.title: Vers une « fuite de données massive » : l’app de vérification de l’âge de l’Europe est criblée de failles
item.description: L'Europe vient de mettre au point son application de vérification de l’âge. Malheureusement, cette app, présentée comme « techniquement prête », est truffée de vulnérabilités préoccupantes. Pour un chercheur, l'app risque de provoquer une « fuite de données massive » à un moment donné.

item.title: Nothing Phone (4a) Pro Review: A Close Second
item.description: It's no Pixel 10a, but the Nothing Phone (4a) Pro is a close second if you're after a midrange phone in the US.

item.title: The Shocking Secrets of Madison Square Garden's Surveillance Machine
item.description: Famously vengeful Knicks owner Jim Dolan has long spied on people at his iconic arenas. WIRED goes deep inside the operation that allegedly tracked a trans woman, lawyers, protesters, and more.

item.title: 'Why gate-keep it?' — Frustrated Marvel fans hit out at the comic book giant as new trailer for Avengers: Doomsday shown behind closed doors at CinemaCon 2026
item.description: Marvel fans are begging for Doomsday's official trailer to be released as numerous AI fakes appear online.

item.title: Two of my favorite prepaid carriers are offering $500 off the Galaxy S26 series right now — but which is the best?
item.description: Both Mint Mobile and Visible Wireless have fantastic deals on the new Galaxy S26 series - but which should you go for?

item.title: This stunning new bridge in Helsinki is designed for cyclists, pedestrians, and trams—but no cars
item.description: At three quarters of a mile in length, the Kruunuvuorensilta, or crown bridge, is likely the longest of its kind in the world. In a new neighborhood in Helsinki, you can skip owning a car. One key part of the district’s design? A new bridge that’s part of the city’s growing bicycle superhighway network.

item.title: With GLP‑1 drug ads everywhere, here’s what to know to safely buy them online
item.description: What to do—and what not to do. If you watched the Super Bowl in 2026, you likely saw Serena Williams share her weight-loss journey on GLP-1 medications in a commercial.

item.title: This Lego-like playground kit is designed for children displaced by war
item.description: Playrise, a new U.K.-based charity, is designing play structures for children living in disaster-relief sites around the world. At the Aysaita Refugee Camp in northeastern Ethiopia’s Afar region, there are about 40,000 Eritreans struggling to meet their basic daily needs. For the 10,000 children younger than 10 who live in the camp, that includes one often overlooked resource: play.At many refugee camps around the world, play can, understandably, become an afterthought as humanitarian organizations focus on delivering essentials like housing and food. But studies show that play is critical fo...
